mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides a seamless local development environment for building and testing containerized applications using Docker and Kubernetes. It simplifies the setup process and integrates with macOS, but it may not be suitable for production workloads due to resource constraints and performance limitations.

steps.title: Download Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step downloads the Docker Desktop application for macOS, which includes Kubernetes support.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

```

> This opens the download page in your default web browser.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step installs Docker Desktop on your macOS system.

```bash

hdiutil attach ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

cp -r /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil detach /Volumes/Docker

```

> Mount the downloaded Docker image.
> Copy Docker.app to your Applications folder.
> Unmount the Docker image.

steps.title: Start Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step launches Docker Desktop and enables Kubernetes.

```bash

open /Applications/Docker.app

sleep 30

osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to activate' -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker" of menu bar 1'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes"'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Apply & Restart"'

```

> This opens Docker Desktop.
> Wait for Docker Desktop to initialize.
> Open Docker preferences.
> Enable Kubernetes in Docker preferences.
> Apply changes and restart Docker.
commands: osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to activate' -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker" of menu bar 1'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes"'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Apply & Restart"'

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if Kubernetes is running correctly within Docker Desktop.

```bash

kubectl version --short

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the Kubernetes client and server versions.
> Verify that the Kubernetes nodes are in a Ready state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description:
This step removes Docker Desktop from your macOS system. Be aware that this will delete all local containers and images.

```bash

osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to quit'

rm -rf /Applications/Docker.app

rm -rf ~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker

```

> Ensure Docker is not running.
> Remove Docker application.
> Remove Docker data.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Docker images and containers. The upgrade process will replace the existing application with the new version.

```bash

docker save -o ~/docker-images.tar $(docker images -q)

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

hdiutil attach ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

cp -r /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil detach /Volumes/Docker

open /Applications/Docker.app

```

> Backup all Docker images.
> Download the latest version of Docker Desktop.
> Mount the downloaded Docker image.
> Copy the new Docker.app to your Applications folder.
> Unmount the Docker image.
> Start the upgraded Docker Desktop.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to Docker Desktop.

**Fix:**
Increase CPU and memory allocation in Docker Desktop settings.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes returns 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes components are not fully initialized.

**Fix:**
Wait a few minutes and check again; ensure Docker Desktop is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using Homebrew: 'brew install kubectl'.

troubleshooting.title: Docker Desktop crashes on startup
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Corrupted installation or incompatible macOS version.

**Fix:**
Reinstall Docker Desktop and ensure your macOS is updated.

versionNotes.changes: Improved Kubernetes integration and added support for Kubernetes 1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME                 STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION' followed by your node details when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to activate' -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker" of menu bar 1'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes"'

prerequisites.description: Ensure your macOS meets the minimum requirements for Docker Desktop.
